materialChanges: Completed the confounder model by adding the causal-pathway exclusion across the objective, learner explanation, worked and independent practice, recovery guidance, claim ledger, glossary, and assessment criteria; added a brief mediator near-miss so the third discriminator is usable rather than merely stated.

Reframed the fictional 70-versus-74 control example around the stated failed negative-control baseline criterion and explicitly withheld any claim of separation without a threshold and variability evidence.

Aligned ATTRIBUTION.md to lesson version 0.1.1, listed all three facts-only sources and their recorded access routes, and replaced obsolete finalizer disclosure with this fresh run ID and supplied instruction digest.

Updated lesson-level AI-assistance disclosure to identify the one-pass revision and adjudication run while preserving lesson identity, version, status, source-confidence state, review artifacts, and all previously sound instruction and assessment material.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The final lesson now requires three confounder discriminators: a separate rival factor rather than a consequence on the intended pathway, systematic variation with the independent variable, and a plausible effect on the dependent variable. The objective, claim, glossary, all operational checks, assessment prompts and rubrics, and recovery guidance agree, and the indicator-binding and fan-airflow examples explicitly distinguish a mediator from a confounder.

The second worked control pattern now concludes only that the negative control failed its stated near-baseline acceptance rule. It expressly says that 70 and 74 alone do not establish separation without a predefined threshold and measurement-variability evidence.

The correct independent, dependent, and controlled-variable distinctions and the sound positive- and negative-control logic were preserved. The lesson and assessment still withhold a target-absent interpretation after a failed positive control and retain explicit limits on what controls establish.
ATTRIBUTION.md now declares version 0.1.1, matching lesson.json and the structured pack artifacts.

The novel acoustics transfer item was retained. Its microphone assignment remains distinct from the guided nitrate-sensor case, and the academic repair strengthens its confounder justification without reducing its transfer demand.

The two supplied independent-practice comparisons and their relationship-level self-check remain performable and informative; only the confounder criterion was extended to include the accepted pathway discriminator.
All four assessment items remain open-response tasks aligned with their analytic rubrics, so each required role and explanation is separately observable.
The prerequisite retrieval check still names and links PREM-SCI-001 and PREM-SCI-002, prompts retrieval before feedback, and provides an immediate reconstruction exercise plus a later recovery route.
The compact-audit prompt remains an explicit check directive with its feedback under a later heading, preserving retrieval before answer reveal in the semantic source.

evidenceChecked: Inspected the authoritative four-scene source order and every scene: each has one level-one heading, logical descending subheadings, prompt-before-feedback reading order, and prose equivalents for every table relationship.

Confirmed that all learning and assessment operations are text-first, untimed, retakeable, and completable through prose, bullets, or tables; no distinction depends on color, position, sound, motion, hover, dragging, speed, physical laboratory work, field sampling, equipment access, or personal data.

Confirmed that the added mediator boundary is defined in learner prose and glossary text, assessed with explicit language rather than a visual-only causal diagram, and supported by the same-scene source notes and the mapped confounding source.

Checked lesson.json and pack contents for assets and media: thirdPartyAssets is empty, no asset or dataset is present, and ATTRIBUTION.md identifies the prose, cases, values, tables, prompts, feedback, assessment, and glossary as original CC BY 4.0 contributions.

Checked every references.json record for creator, title, publisher, date or version, exact HTTPS route, locator, use type, facts-only-original-expression basis, rights evidence, and dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found record; the CC BY-NC article is used only for facts, with no third-party expression or figure reused.

Checked claims.json against learner-visible source notes: each material claim is mapped to all four claim-bearing scenes, every source note names all mapped claim and source IDs, and the revised confounder claim states its scope, uncertainty, and causal-pathway boundary.

Confirmed accountable author and finalizer disclosure, fresh finalizer run ID, supplied sha256 instruction digest, CC BY 4.0 contribution statement, absence of license exceptions, and explicit statement that provenance is operator-accountable.

Statically checked the pack for normalized local paths, raw HTML, scripts, event handlers, iframes, remote embeds, Markdown images, executable content, unsafe instructions, secrets, and identifiable learner, patient, participant, or environmental records; none is present.
limitations: No disabled learner or human assistive-technology user participated, and no rendered browser, screen-reader, keyboard-traversal, 200% zoom, reduced-motion, or print session was run; this audit is based on the complete semantic Markdown and structured JSON source.
PDF, EPUB, DOCX, and JATS exporters are documented as unavailable, so their output could not be tested; canonical source was checked for a continuous text-first route and explicit equivalents.

The audit relied on the pack's dated source-access and rights records and the eligible academic review's source checks; substantive source pages and current platform terms were not independently reopened in this run.

The task expressly prohibited reading site/, running npm, or using repository scripts, so the public schemas, strict source preflight, generated renderer, and repository validator were not executed. JSON parsing, internal cross-link checks, source-note coverage checks, prohibited-content scans, and diff checks were performed directly on the pack instead.
No real learner or independent human subject-matter expert tested the final revision, so no claim is made about measured comprehension, mastery rates, legal certainty, or human verification.

rationale: The original candidate is PREM-SCI-003 v0.1.1 at 068f88b1e9081d01477e6f0fbfc5d94b96b351bc under standard-lesson-v3. The two counted advisory artifacts target that exact lesson, version, and commit: one academic request-changes record from the declared OpenAI run and one learning-design approve record from the declared Google run. They use distinct run IDs and declared providers, both disclose the founding-stage principal conflict, and the operator reports equivalent non-dismissed COMMENTED GitHub transports. This run recomputed two review inputs, one approval, one change request, one academic and one learning-design role, one principal, two agent runs, and two declared model families; these are internally consistent, attested fields rather than independently verified provider identity. The two older pack-local reviews were excluded because they target version 0.1.0 and commit 2e08cf491b13cc51322fb535c4ae116b05ccd124. Every counted finding is disposed above. The one-pass revision incorporates both academic corrections and the attribution mismatch while preserving the learning-design strengths, and the static accessibility-and-rights audit found no unresolved content, access, safety, privacy, or reuse blocker. The required final SHA and runtime banner stamping do not yet exist, and repository/schema validation was prohibited in this run; the merge decision is therefore contingent on the operator completing those mechanical checks without changing teaching content.
dissent: The academic input requested changes because the candidate's two-part confounder rule could misclassify a mediator and because one control-value comparison overclaimed separation; both findings were incorporated, but the original dissent remains material context for the final revision.

The same accountable principal, github:VSBDev, is the lesson principal and operates both reviewers and this finalizer. The policy permits this founding-stage conflict, but it provides no principal-level independence and the disclosed run/provider diversity is attested rather than platform-verified.

The academic reviewer could not open the complete confounding article because of a reCAPTCHA barrier and used an indexed extract for the causal-pathway definition; the finalizer did not reopen source pages, so source and rights verification retains that limitation.
No human learner, subject-matter expert, accessibility specialist, or legal reviewer examined the final revision, and no rendered assistive-technology path was exercised.
conditions: The operator must commit the unchanged eligible reviews with this single final content revision, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character commit SHA, and stamp the four RUNTIME-STAMPED runtime fields from the runtime banner without changing teaching content.

Before merge, the operator must run the strict source preflight and repository validation required by the project, validate adjudication.json against the public schema, and resolve any failure; this run did not and does not claim those checks passed.

After the recorded final commit, only the documented maintainer publication transition may change lesson status, sourceConfidence, and retained claim reviewStatus fields; any other teaching-content change makes this adjudication stale.
This merge value records the finalizer's evidence-backed decision only; it does not authorize this agent to commit, push, merge, or publish.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — standard lesson finalizer and adjudicator
